title: Cuitlauzina egertonii
intro: Cuitlauzina egertonii, also known as Mexican Prickly Poppy, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Cuitlauzina egertonii is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
